title: Try to enable "run" tests in CI
body: Try to ensure that we have runc, so that we can test the `run` command in CI.

comments.body: LGTM, but I think I'm losing it, thought this had been done already.
Ugh, gotta stop clicking too quickly today, my emergency mouse isn't as responsive as my normal mouse that I forgot in the office yesterday.  Apologies for the close.
@TomSweeneyRedHat no worries.  I just realized that we were skipping most of the tests in `run.bats` because the CI testers didn't have `runc` installed.
![](https://github.trello.services/images/mini-trello-icon.png) [(3) [buildah] Run tests that run runc, in CI](https://trello.com/c/WLwEY8IZ/585-3-buildah-run-tests-that-run-runc-in-ci)
Would we still test on travis to cover ubuntu use cases?
Yes.  We need to supply our own `runc`, though, since a packaged, compatible version may not be around.

@rh-atomic-bot r+ https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/202/commits/cb6ab6428d59c234f4f7dceb5780d87adc3a3f7e
LGTM More testing is good.
